name: Debian
description: Established by Ian Murdock in August 1993. Debian is developed openly by a team of volunteers.

name: Fedora
description: Cutting-edge, Red Hat sponsored, latest GNOME.

name: Arch Linux
description: Rolling release, minimalist, pacman + AUR, excellent wiki.

name: Linux Mint
description: Beginner-friendly, non corp, funded via donations.

name: Zorin OS
description: Beginner-friendly distribution developed by Zorin Group designed to make your computer faster.

name: openSUSE
description: A Linux distribution developed by the openSUSE Project. It is offered in two main variations.

name: Ubuntu
description: Popular and reliable Linux distribution developed by the British company Canonical and a community of contributors.

name: Kubuntu
description: User friendly Ubuntu-based distro launched in 2004, developed by its community team, previously sponsored by Blue Systems and Canonical.

name: NixOS
description: A Linux distribution built around the Nix package manager.

name: CachyOS
description: A modern, user-friendly and independent desktop-focused Linux distribution built from scratch.

name: Solus
description: A modern, user-friendly and independent desktop-focused Linux distribution built from scratch.

name: AnduinOS
description: Lightweight, beginner friendly Ubuntu-based Linux distribution that offers a familiar and easy-to-use experience for anyone moving to Linux.

name: TUXEDO OS
description: Ubuntu-based, great for gaming & creators, hybrid rolling release updating kernel and de.

name: EndeavourOS
description: Arch with easy graphical installer, close to vanilla.

name: Manjaro
description: User-friendly Arch with Calamares, multiple DE editions.

name: Nobara
description: Fedora-based gaming distro with preconfigured drivers.

name: Endless OS
description: A Safe, User-Friendly Operating System Packed with Educational Tools, Games, and Pathways To Turn Your Youth Into Creators.
